title: Ka'rta Tor
content:
{{Top|leg|italics=1}} {{Youmay|a Mandalorian war chant in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity|[[Ka'rta]], a Mandalorian hexagonal symbol in ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]], or [[Iron Heart|ka'rta beskar]] in ''Star Wars'' Legends}} "'''Ka'rta Tor'''," meaning "One Heart of Justice," was an ancient [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] war chant sung in [[Mando'a/Legends|Mando'a]]. During his training of the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] revised a number of chants, including "[[Gra'tua Cuun]]," replacing traditional Mandalorian keywords with more appropriate Republic ones. In this instance, it is presumable that he replaced "the Mandalorians'" with "The Jedi." The chant was sung by the clone troopers with the accompaniment of drums and horns. ==Lyrics== ===[[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']]=== ''Kandosii sa kyr'am ast,
'' ''Troan teroch jetiise a'den,
'' ''Duraan vi at ara'nov.
'' ''Vode an, ka'rta tor.
'' ''Kote.
'' ===[[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]=== As ruthless as Death itself,
The pitiless face of The Jedi's wrath,
Let us look down on all who are before us.
Brothers all, one heart of justice.
Glory.
(Can also be translated as "The pitiless face of the Republic's wrath") ==Behind the scenes== According to the [[Mando'a/Legends|Mando'a]] dictionary, the proper spelling is ''kar'ta'', which means "heart."{{WebCite|url=http://www.karentraviss.com/page20/page26/index.html|text=Mando'a|author=Karen Traviss|work=[http://www.karentraviss.com Karen Traviss]|archivedate=20110925083955}} ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic: Knights of the Fallen Empire]]'' *''[[Star Wars: Republic Commando]]'' {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{LucasArts|url=games/swrepubliccommando/downloads/mp3s/Lyrics.pdf|text="Star Wars: Republic Commando: English Translation of Ancient Mandalorian Text"}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Ka'rta Tor |es=Ka'rta Tor |pl=Ka'rta Tor }} [[Category:Mandalorian chants and songs]]
